revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Azusamiten-jinja (Mizuho)]][[ja:阿豆佐味天神社]] {{Expand Japanese|阿豆佐味天神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Azusami Tenjinsha | native_name = 阿豆佐味天神社 | image = Azusamiten-jinja (Mizuho), haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Azusami Tenjinsha | coordinates = {{coord|35.783111|139.150076}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|ca|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|fr|Sukunabikona|it|Sukuna-hikona|ja|スクナビコナ|ko|스쿠나비코나|mni|ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯕꯤꯀꯣꯅ|pl|Sukunabikona|pt|Sukunahikona|simple|Sukunabikona|sv|Suku-Na-Bikona|zh|少彥名|qid=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}} }} {{nihongo|'''Azusami Tenjinsha'''|阿豆佐味天神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} * {{ill|Q798838|ltq=Q798838|qid=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 8 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039728|ltq=Q135039728|qid=Q135039728|1=Ohomatonotsuno- Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135039729|ltq=Q135039729|qid=Q135039729|1=Anasahano- Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Mizuho, Tokyo|lt=Mizuho|arz|ميزوهو|ca|Mizuho (Tòquio)|ceb|Mizuho-machi|de|Mizuho (Tokio)|en|Mizuho, Tokyo|es|Mizuho (Tokio)|fa|میزوهو، توکیو|fi|Mizuho (Tokio)|fr|Mizuho (Tokyo)|hy|Միձուհո (ավան)|id|Mizuho, Tōkyō|it|Mizuho (Tokyo)|ja|瑞穂町|javoyage|瑞穂町|ka|მიძუჰო (დასახლება)|ko|미즈호정|ms|Mizuho, Tokyo|my|မိဇုဟိုမြို့၊ တိုကျို|no|Mizuho|pt|Mizuho (Tóquio)|ro|Mizuho, Tōkyō|ru|Мидзухо (посёлок)|sv|Mizuho, Tokyo|th|มิซูโฮะ (โตเกียว)|tl|Mizuho, Tokyo|tr|Mizuho, Tokyo|uk|Мідзухо (Токіо)|vi|Mizuho, Tokyo|zh_yue|瑞穗町|zh|瑞穗町|qid=Q975797|1=Mizuho, Tokyo}} * {{ill|Q907398|ltq=Q907398|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.783111|139.150076}} == image (P18) == * Azusamiten-jinja (Mizuho), haiden.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q3275687|ltq=Q3275687|qid=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}} * {{ill|Q1753428|ltq=Q1753428|qid=Q1753428|1=Tenjin (kami)}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181696 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|lt=nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|fr|Nagare-zukuri|it|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|pl|Nagare-zukuri|ru|Нагарэ-дзукури|sv|Nagare-zukuri|zh|流造|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2013105000110 == Commons category (P373) == * Azusamiten-jinja (Mizuho) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '東京都西多摩郡瑞穂町殿ヶ谷宮前1008[1]', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '東京都西多摩郡瑞穂町殿ケ谷', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Azusamiten-jinja (Mizuho)}} [[JA:阿豆佐味天神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11657681}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=January 2015}} {{otheruses||The shrine of the same name in Sunagawa-chō, Tachikawa City, Tokyo|Azusami Tenjinsha (Sunagawa-chō, Tachikawa City)}} {{Shrine |Name = Azusami Tenjinsha |Image = [[File:Azusamiten-jinja (Mizuho), haiden.jpg|270px|]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = 1008 Tonegaya Miyamae, {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|qid=Q1048689|1=Nishitama District}}{{ill|lt=Mizuho Town|Q975797|qid=Q975797|1=Mizuho, Tokyo}}{{sfn|Tokyo Metropolitan Association of Shinto Shrines}} |Coordinates= {{Coord|35|46|0.52|N|139|21|53.65|E|type:landmark_region:JP-13|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}} |Status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}| |Founded = (Traditionally) {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|qid=Q1346292|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=892|Q30593|qid=Q30593|1=892}}){{sfn|Tokyo Metropolitan Association of Shinto Shrines}} |Main Hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other name = |Pilgrimage = |Festival = {{ill|lt=March 4|Q2396|qid=Q2396|1=March 4}}{{sfn|Tokyo Metropolitan Association of Shinto Shrines}} |Rites = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Azusami Tenjinsha|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|0.52|N|139|21|53.65|E}}}} }} [[File:Azusamiten-jinja (Mizuho), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Azusami Tenjinsha''' (あづさみてんじんじゃ{{sfn|Tokyo Metropolitan Association of Shinto Shrines}}) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Mizuho Town|Q975797|qid=Q975797|1=Mizuho, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|qid=Q1048689|1=Nishitama District}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the head shrine of all Azusami Tenjinsha. This ancient shrine stands at the southern foothills of the {{ill|lt=Sayama Hills|Q11571728|qid=Q11571728|1=Sayama Hills}}, almost on the boundary between Mizuho Town and {{ill|lt=Musashimurayama City|Q390806|qid=Q390806|1=Musashimurayama, Tokyo}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as one of the eight shrines of {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|qid=Q11430789|1=Tama district}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}{{efn|These eight are: {{ill|lt=Akiru Shrine|Q11657213|qid=Q11657213|1=Akiru Shrine}} (Akiruno City); Ono Shrine (both {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama City)|Q11464224|qid=Q11464224|lt=Tama City|1=Ono Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Fuchū City)|Q11464226|qid=Q11464226|lt=Fuchū City|1=Ono Shrine (Fuchū)}}, regarded as {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}); {{ill|lt=Fuda Tenjinsha|Q11480773|qid=Q11480773|1=Fudaten Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Chōfu City|Q210667|qid=Q210667|1=Chōfu}}); {{ill|lt=Musashi Mitake Shrine|Q6587276|qid=Q6587276|lt=Mitake Shrine|1=Musashi-Mitake Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Ōme City|Q237683|qid=Q237683|1=Ōme, Tokyo}}); '''Azusami Tenjinsha'''; {{ill|lt=Anazawa Tenjinsha|Q11597242|qid=Q11597242|1=Anazawa Tenjinsha}} ({{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|qid=Q317847|1=Inagi}}); {{ill|lt=Torakashi Shrine|Q11625297|qid=Q11625297|1=Torakashi Shrine}} (Ōme City); and {{ill|lt=Aoi Shrine|Q11662605|qid=Q11662605|1=Aoi Shrine}} (Chōfu City, Ōme City, Inagi City).}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are the following three{{sfn|Tokyo Metropolitan Association of Shinto Shrines}}: * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687}} (すくなひこなのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}} (おおなむちのみこと) === History === [[File:Azusamiten-jinja (Mizuho), shaden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine buildings}}]] According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|qid=Q1346292|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=892|Q30593|qid=Q30593|1=892}}) by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prince Takamochi|1=UNKNOWN}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kanmu Heishi|1=UNKNOWN}} clan{{sfn|Tokyo Metropolitan Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It served as the chief {{ill|lt=guardian shrine|Q135014617|qid=Q135014617|lt=chinju|1=BadSmith}} of Murayama-gō and was revered as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|qid=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of the {{ill|lt=Murayama clan|Q11523092|qid=Q11523092|1=Murayama clan}}, one of the {{ill|lt=Seven Musashi Bands|Q11546323|qid=Q11546323|1=Seven Musashi Bands}}, who were descendants of Prince Takamochi and part of the Chichibu Heishi lineage. Various theories exist regarding the shrine’s name “Azusami”: that it derives from the {{ill|lt=azusa bow|Q4833134|qid=Q4833134|1=Azusa Yumi}}, from the {{ill|lt=kisasage tree|Q1144805|qid=Q1144805|lt=kisasage|1=Catalpa ovata}} (ancient name: azusa), or from a {{ill|lt=spring|Q1881858|qid=Q1881858|1=spring water}}—“Azu” meaning {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sweet|1=UNKNOWN}}, “sa” as a prefix, and “mi” meaning {{ill|lt=water|Q283|qid=Q283|1=Water}}, thus “sweet water.” After repairs in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1584|Q6728|qid=Q6728|1=1584}}) and {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1598|Q6800|qid=Q6800|1=1598}}), the shrine buildings were restored during the {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|qid=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} era ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|qid=Q6892|1=1716}}–{{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|qid=Q6989|1=1736}}) by Murayama Tosa-no-kami. Thereafter, the {{ill|lt=Later Hōjō clan|Q431928|qid=Q431928|1=Later Hōjō clan}} donated shrine lands worth 15 {{ill|lt=kan|Q116911247|qid=Q116911247|1=Japanese kan}} {{ill|lt=mon (currency)|Q6279611|qid=Q6279611|lt=mon|1=Chinese cash (currency unit)}}, and the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|1=UNKNOWN}} granted 12 {{ill|lt=koku|Q855048|qid=Q855048|1=Koku}} as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shuinchi|1=UNKNOWN}}. Thus, it was generously patronized by successive {{ill|lt=lords|Q2366336|qid=Q2366336|1=Seigneur}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}) it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. Incidentally, the place name Tonegaya is said to derive from the location of the Murayama clan’s residence. In front of the shrine stands a shrine-name stele dated {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|qid=Q730266|1=Bunkyū}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1863|Q7701|qid=Q7701|1=1863}}). The {{ill|lt=Azusami Tenjinsha (Sunagawa-chō, Tachikawa City)|Q77267791|qid=Q77267791|lt=Azusami Tenjinsha|1=Azusami Tenjinsha}} located in {{ill|lt=Sunagawa-chō (Tachikawa City)|Q11587432|qid=Q11587432|1=Sunagawachō}}, {{ill|lt=Tachikawa City|Q269634|qid=Q269634|1=Tachikawa, Tokyo}}, was invited as the guardian shrine during the development of the area that later became {{ill|lt=Sunagawa Village|Q11587431|qid=Q11587431|1=Sunagawa}} after {{ill|lt=1629|Q6847|qid=Q6847|1=1629}} ({{ill|lt=Kan’ei|Q1192913|qid=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 6), and its main hall was built in {{ill|lt=1738|Q6996|qid=Q6996|1=1738}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|qid=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 3){{Cite book|Japanese|author= |title=Tokyo Shrine Directory, Vol. 2 |publisher=Tokyo Metropolitan Association of Shinto Shrines |date=1986-03 |pages=324–325 |isbn=}}{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://azusami-suitengu.net/?page_id=237 |title=Azusami Tenjinsha / Tachikawa Suitengū – History and Origins |publisher=Azusami Tenjinsha |accessdate=2019-12-01}}. The Murano family of Kishi Village (present-day {{ill|lt=Kishi (Musashimurayama City)|Q11475773|qid=Q11475773|lt=Kishi|1=Kishi}}, {{ill|lt=Musashimurayama City|Q390806|qid=Q390806|1=Musashimurayama, Tokyo}}), who developed the new fields of {{ill|lt=Sunagawa|Q11587432|qid=Q11587432|1=Sunagawachō}}, later became known as the Sunagawa family and claimed descent from the {{ill|lt=Murayama clan|Q11523092|qid=Q11523092|1=Murayama clan}}. In addition, {{ill|lt=Kodaira Shinmei Shrine|Q43594496|qid=Q43594496|1=Kodaira Shinmei-gu}} in {{ill|lt=Ogawa-chō (Kodaira City)|Q11460543|qid=Q11460543|lt=Ogawa-chō|1=Ogawachō}}, {{ill|lt=Kodaira City|Q213278|qid=Q213278|1=Kodaira, Tokyo}}[http://kodaira-shinmeigu.jp/ Kodaira Shinmei Shrine], and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Naka-chō (Kodaira City)|Q119657977|qid=Q119657977|lt=Naka-chō|1=Nakamachi}}[http://kumanogu.com/ Kumano Shrine] were invited by the Ogawa clan of Kishi Village—developers of Ogawa Shinden—and by the shrine priests of this shrine, the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyazaki clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, from this shrine’s {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|qid=Q135009904|1=Sessha}}: Shinmei Shrine and Kumano Shrine, which had been enshrined in Kishi Village. === Subsidiary shrines === * Okumiya, Shinmei Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Raijin Shrine, Shōichi-i Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine, Suga Shrine, Kōshi Shrine File:Azusamiten-jinja (Mizuho), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shrines within the precincts File:Azusamiten-jinja (Mizuho), keidaisha-2.jpg|Shrines within the precincts === Grand festival === * {{ill|lt=March 4|Q2396|qid=Q2396|1=March 4}} === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Hakonegasaki Station|Q844354|qid=Q844354|lt=Hakonegasaki Station East Exit|1=Hakonegasaki Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hachikō Line|Q1372071|qid=Q1372071|1=Hachikō Line}}, take a {{ill|lt=Tachikawa Bus|Q11597871|qid=Q11597871|1=Tachikawa Bus}} bound for {{ill|lt=Tachikawa Station|Q801501|qid=Q801501|1=Tachikawa Station}} North Exit or {{ill|lt=Akishima Station|Q844152|qid=Q844152|lt=Akishima Station North Exit|1=Akishima Station}}, get off at Tonegaya, then walk 3 minutes. * From {{ill|lt=Tachikawa Station|Q801501|qid=Q801501|1=Tachikawa Station}} North Exit on the {{ill|lt=Chūō Line (Rapid)|Q1191297|qid=Q1191297|lt=Chūō Line|1=Chūō Line (Rapid)}} and others, take a Tachikawa Bus bound for Hakonegasaki Station East Exit or Iwakura Kaidō Iriguchi, get off at Tonegaya, then walk 3 minutes. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuho Town Community Bus|1=UNKNOWN}}, get off at Yoriai House Ikoi, then walk 3 minutes. (Route between Hakonegasaki Station East Exit and Ishihata–Tonegaya) * {{ill|lt=Musashimurayama City Circular Bus|Q11546365|qid=Q11546365|1=MM Shuttle}}, get off at Mitsugi District Community Center, then walk 8 minutes. (Route between {{ill|lt=Kamikitadai Station|Q6357916|qid=Q6357916|1=Kamikitadai Station}}) === Notes === '''Annotations''' {{Notelist}} '''References''' === Further reading === * {{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nishitama/mizuho/5503/|title=Azusami Tenjinsha|publisher=Tokyo Metropolitan Association of Shinto Shrines|accessdate=2020-7-12|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|Japanese|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|qid=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol. 120, Tama District No. 32|chapter=Tonegaya Village, Azusami Tenjinsha|id={{NDLJP|763994/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Home Ministry|date=1884-6}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Azusamiten-jinja (Mizuho)}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Azusami Tenjinsha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Azusami Tenjinsha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Azusami Tenjinsha]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:瑞穂町の建築物]] [[Category:瑞穂町の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]]
